description_long: This bollard consists of three distinct vertical columns, each with a unique design, arranged side by side on square bases. The leftmost column features a spiraled, segmented shaft with a square cap and base, giving it a sculptural, almost twisted appearance. The central column is more angular, with a tapered shaft and pronounced geometric transitions, topped with a flat, blocky cap. The rightmost column is fluted, reminiscent of classical architecture, with two horizontal bands encircling the shaft and a flat square cap. All three columns are made of a stone-like material with a weathered, rough texture, and their surfaces show signs of aging and patina. The overall coloration is a mottled grayish-brown, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. These bollards are robust and decorative, suitable for demarcating pedestrian zones or protecting urban infrastructure.
description: A set of three robust, decorative stone bollards with unique column designs and weathered, grayish-brown surfaces, each mounted on a square base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spiraled column in the center, with its segmented, twisted design and square cap most prominent.
description_view_1: Side view displaying all three columns: the spiraled, the angular-tapered, and the fluted classical column, each with distinct textures and shapes.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the fluted column in the center, with its vertical grooves and horizontal bands clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing all three columns again, with the fluted column now on the left and the spiraled column on the right.
attribute_cap_design: flat square cap with slight decorative features
